exactly_identical.annotation:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the court
TO specify the activity and the person providing the activity in the direction
WHEN issuing an activity direction
exactly_identical.groundtruth:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the condition
TO specify the activity and the person providing the activity.

same_meaning_substantial_paraphrase.annotation:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR an individual who is a party to the proceedings
TO take part in an activity that would help to establish, maintain or improve the involvement in the life of the child concerned
WHEN the court issues an activity direction
same_meaning_substantial_paraphrase.groundtruth:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a child arrangements order
TO impose a condition (an "activity condition") requiring an individual falling within subsection (3) to take part in an activity that would, in the court's opinion, help to establish, maintain or improve the involvement in the life of the child concerned of— (a) that individual, or (b) another individual who is a party to the proceedings.

same_norm_different_meaning.annotation:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR the court
TO make an activity direction in connection with the provision the court is considering whether to make
WHEN the court is considering whether to make provision about one or more of the matters mentioned in subsection (1A) [source: section-11A(1)]
same_norm_different_meaning.groundtruth:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR A court
TO make an activity direction under section 11A(2) in connection with any matter mentioned in section 11A(1A)
UNLESS there is a dispute as regards the provision about that matter that the court is considering whether to make in the proceedings. [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11A]

same_norm_different_meaning.annotation:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR the court
TO make an activity direction in connection with the provision of the child arrangements order
WHEN a person has failed to comply with a provision of a child arrangements order or what steps to take in consequence of a person's failure to comply [source: section-11A(2A)]
same_norm_different_meaning.groundtruth:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR a court
TO on the same occasion, — (a) make an activity direction under subsection (2B), and (b) dispose finally of the proceedings as they relate to failure to comply with the provision in connection with which the activity direction is made.

same_norm_different_meaning.annotation:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR the court
TO require an individual by an activity direction to take part in mediation
WHEN issuing an activity direction
same_norm_different_meaning.groundtruth:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR A court
TO make an activity direction in relation to an individual
UNLESS the individual is habitually resident in England and Wales [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11A]

same_norm_different_meaning.annotation:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the court
TO consider the welfare of the child as the paramount consideration
WHEN considering whether to make an activity direction
same_norm_different_meaning.groundtruth:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a court
TO make an activity direction in connection with that provision of the child arrangements order.

only_in_annotations.annotation:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR the court
TO require an individual by an activity direction to undergo medical or psychiatric examination, assessment or treatment
WHEN issuing an activity direction

IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR the court
TO make an activity direction under subsection (2) and dispose finally of the proceedings as they relate to the matters mentioned in subsection (1A) on the same occasion
WHEN the activity direction is made in connection with the matters mentioned in subsection (1A)

IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR the court
TO make an activity direction under subsection (2B) and dispose finally of the proceedings as they relate to failure to comply with the provision on the same occasion
WHEN the activity direction is made in connection with the failure to comply with the provision

only_in_groundtruth.groundtruth:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR A court
TO make an activity direction requiring an individual who is a child to take part in an activity
UNLESS the individual is a parent of the child in relation to whom the court is considering provision about a matter mentioned in section 11A(1A). [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11A]

IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR A court
TO make an activity activity direction in connection with the making, variation or discharge of a child arrangements order
WHEN/IF/WHERE the child arrangements order is, or would if made be, an excepted order. [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11A]

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a child arrangements order
TO be varied so as to impose a condition (an "activity condition") requiring an individual falling within subsection (3) to take part in an activity that would, in the court's opinion, help to establish, maintain or improve the involvement in the life of the child concerned of— (a) that individual, or (b) another individual who is a party to the proceedings.
